title: The 25 Best Movies of the Century: No. 17 - ‘Anchorman: The Legend of Ron Burgundy’
format: Sean Fennessey and Amanda Dobbins put Anchorman at No. 17 on their 25-best-of-the-century list, the only studio comedy on it, and use it to walk through the Ferrell and McKay partnership and the Apatow-era comedy boom it kicked off.

segments.heading: No. 17: ‘Anchorman: The Legend of Ron Burgundy’

films.title: Anchorman: The Legend of Ron Burgundy
films.note: Sean's favorite comedy of the century and Adam McKay's directorial debut, co-written with and starring Will Ferrell off the back of their SNL run. Sean had blocked three hours to rewatch a movie he remembered as bloated Apatow-era improv and found a 90-minute machine instead. The hosts argue it earns the slot because the men are at once the subject and the butt of the sexism joke, a hard trick to pull off, and because Christina Applegate's Veronica Corningstone gives it a real female lead. Ferrell as the full-of-it man-baby, the Baxter/Jack Black burrito hinge, Steve Carell and Paul Rudd's Brian Fantana, Fred Willard and a young Kathryn Hahn round out the case. It beat Superbad, which led at the start.

segments.heading: The comedies it beat
segments.note: the contenders for the one comedy slot

films.title: Superbad
films.note: The early leader in the clubhouse before a couple of pivots. Both still feel bad leaving it, and Jonah Hill entirely, off the list.

films.title: Step Brothers
films.note: The only other film Sean seriously weighed. Funnier as pure performance art, they say, but thinner thematically and with nothing like Applegate's role.

films.title: Mean Girls
films.note: A contender and another SNL-to-screen crossover (Tina Fey's this time) that didn't make the cut.

films.title: 21 Jump Street
films.note: Very well liked by both, very funny, the other Jonah Hill omission. He'll be back on the list later.

films.title: Tropic Thunder
films.note: Amanda's coldest of the bunch. Sean grants its importance and its Hearts of Darkness roots but not the cultural centrality Anchorman had.

films.title: The Hangover
films.note: A box-office tentpole of the era, still funny to Sean, but a mean-spirited movie where everyone's an asshole, the opposite of Anchorman's tinge of sweetness.

films.title: Something's Gotta Give
films.note: The list's other comedy, a different script-and-production-design kind of film. The hosts pair the two as movies about men and women failing to communicate.

films.title: A Night at the Roxbury
films.note: Ferrell's first co-written vehicle, a stretched-out club sketch that didn't work, the wrong container for what Sean calls his essence.

films.title: Austin Powers: International Man of Mystery
films.note: An early Ferrell cameo (Mustafa, at the bottom of the trapdoor: 'I'm badly hurt') the hosts flag as a setup for his screen persona, after briefly mixing up Mugatu and Zoolander.

films.title: Old School
films.note: Already a hit a year before Anchorman. Sean rates Vince Vaughn's Frank the Tank, the true Belushi of the movie, as the best part.

films.title: Elf
films.note: A massive hit Sean didn't even consider for the list. Ferrell as the sweet, harmless overgrown child, the flip side of Chevy Chase's asshole.

films.title: The Other Guys
films.note: Sean's turning point for McKay: the first film that's secretly a manifesto (the financial crisis) hiding under a buddy comedy.

films.title: The Big Short
films.note: Sean's favorite of McKay's message movies: manifesto, ensemble, and structural audacity, Margot Robbie in the bathtub, all at once.

films.title: Anchorman 2: The Legend Continues
films.note: Sean liked it more than the consensus 'been there, done that', a pointed text on cable news that reads sharper now.

films.title: Don't Look Up
films.note: Took its lumps but ranks among Netflix's most-watched films, proof McKay hasn't become an obscure filmmaker.

films.title: Network
films.note: The hosts' comp: a '70s newsroom, corporate incursion, a woman on the rise. Applegate's styling, that first white pantsuit, reads as a deliberate Faye Dunaway / Diana Christensen reference. Sean's kind of comedy, the horrified laugh, versus Anchorman's laughing-together.

films.title: The Naked Gun
films.note: A month out at recording, the reboot they frame as the current test of whether a theatrical studio comedy with stars still lands with anyone under 22.

films: Talladega Nights: The Ballad of Ricky Bobby

referenced.group: The studio-comedy canon they rattle off
